description: FutSpring Pelada Management — create and manage recurring football groups, player membership, admin actions, and home page

userStories.title: Backend Pelada entity and database schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Pelada JPA entity and database tables so that pelada data can be stored and retrieved.
acceptanceCriteria: Pelada entity with fields: id (Long, auto), name (required), dayOfWeek (String, required), timeOfDay (String, required), duration (Float, required), address (String, nullable), reference (String, nullable), image (String, nullable), autoCreateDailyEnabled (boolean, default false), createdAt
Many-to-many relationship: Pelada has a Set<User> members and Set<User> admins (separate join tables: pelada_members, pelada_admins)
PeladaRepository extends JpaRepository
PeladaResponseDTO with fields: id, name, dayOfWeek, timeOfDay, duration, address, reference, image, autoCreateDailyEnabled, memberCount
Hibernate DDL auto creates the tables
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend Pelada CRUD endpoints: create, list my peladas, get detail
userStories.description: As a developer, I need REST endpoints to create a pelada, list the current user's peladas, and fetch pelada detail so the frontend can display them.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/peladas — creates pelada; authenticated user becomes creator and first admin and member; returns PeladaResponseDTO
GET /api/v1/peladas/my — returns list of all peladas the current user belongs to
GET /api/v1/peladas/:id — returns pelada detail including members list (id, username, image, stars, position, isAdmin); only accessible to members (403 for non-members)
PeladaDetailResponseDTO includes: pelada fields + members list with isAdmin flag
Request body uses @Valid with required field validation; returns 400 on missing required fields
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend player management endpoints and user search
userStories.description: As a developer, I need endpoints to add/remove players and toggle admin status so admins can manage pelada membership.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/peladas/:id/players — body: { userId }; adds user as member; admin only; 404 if user not found; 409 if already a member
DELETE /api/v1/peladas/:id/players/:userId — removes player from pelada; admin only; 403 if trying to remove the creator
PUT /api/v1/peladas/:id/players/:userId/admin — body: { isAdmin: boolean }; toggles admin status; admin only; 403 if trying to demote creator
GET /api/v1/users/search?q= — case-insensitive search by username or email; returns max 10 results as list of UserResponseDTO; authenticated users only
All endpoints return 403 if caller is not an admin (except search)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend pelada edit, delete, and image upload
userStories.description: As a developer, I need endpoints to update pelada settings, delete a pelada, and upload a pelada image so admins can manage pelada info.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT /api/v1/peladas/:id — updates pelada fields (name, dayOfWeek, timeOfDay, duration, address, reference, autoCreateDailyEnabled); admin only; returns updated PeladaResponseDTO
DELETE /api/v1/peladas/:id — deletes pelada; creator only (403 for non-creator admins); cascades deletion to all related data (members, admins associations)
POST /api/v1/peladas/:id/image — multipart/form-data; stores file under uploads/ with UUID prefix; updates pelada image field; admin only; max 5MB; JPEG/PNG/WebP only
GET /api/v1/files/:filename — serves uploaded files from uploads/ directory (reuse or extend existing file serving if already set up from auth)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend API layer for peladas
userStories.description: As a developer, I need TypeScript types and API functions for pelada endpoints so frontend pages can call the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/types/pelada.ts with interfaces: PeladaMember (id, username, image, stars, position, isAdmin), PeladaResponse (id, name, dayOfWeek, timeOfDay, duration, address, reference, image, autoCreateDailyEnabled, memberCount), PeladaDetail (extends PeladaResponse + members: PeladaMember[])

Create src/api/peladas.ts with functions: getMyPeladas(), getPelada(id), createPelada(data), updatePelada(id, data), deletePelada(id), addPlayer(peladaId, userId), removePlayer(peladaId, userId), setAdmin(peladaId, userId, isAdmin), uploadPeladaImage(peladaId, file), searchUsers(q)
All functions use the existing Axios client (src/api/client.ts) which attaches the Bearer token
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend Home page — My Peladas grid
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all my peladas on the home page so I can quickly navigate to one.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace the /home placeholder with a real page that fetches and displays pelada cards using getMyPeladas()
Each card shows: pelada image (or placeholder), name, dayOfWeek, timeOfDay
Cards link to /pelada/:id
Skeleton loading state while fetching (3–4 skeleton cards)
Empty state message if user has no peladas: 'No peladas yet. Create one to get started!'
NavBar is present at the top (reuse existing NavBar component)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend Create Pelada modal
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create a pelada from the home page so I can organize football sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Create Pelada' button on the home page opens a modal/dialog

Form fields: name (required text), dayOfWeek (required select: Monday–Sunday), timeOfDay (required time input), duration (required number, hours), address (optional text), reference (optional text), autoCreateDailyEnabled (boolean toggle, default false)
Optional image file upload field (JPEG/PNG/WebP, max 5MB); if provided, uploads image after pelada creation using uploadPeladaImage()
On success: close modal, show success toast, navigate to /pelada/:id
On error: show error toast, keep modal open
Submit button shows loading state during API call
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend Pelada detail page — info and player list
userStories.description: As a member, I want to view the pelada's details and player list so I know when, where, and with whom I'm playing.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /pelada/:id page that fetches pelada detail using getPelada(id)
Header section shows: pelada image (or placeholder banner), name, dayOfWeek + timeOfDay, duration, address, reference
Members section shows a list of players: avatar (or initials placeholder), username, position badge, stars (filled star icons), admin badge if isAdmin
Non-member accessing the route sees an 'Access Denied' message (not a crash) — detect 403 from API
Skeleton loading state while fetching
NavBar present at top
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend admin player management UI
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to add, remove, and promote players directly from the pelada page.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Add Player' button visible only to admins; opens a search dialog with a text input that calls searchUsers(q) on change (debounced); selecting a user calls addPlayer() and refetches members

Each player row shows a 'Remove' action (admin only, hidden for non-admins); clicking shows a confirmation dialog; on confirm calls removePlayer() and refetches; creator cannot be removed (hide action for creator)
Each player row shows 'Make Admin' / 'Remove Admin' toggle (admin only); clicking calls setAdmin() and refetches; creator's admin status cannot be changed (hide action for creator)
All admin actions show success/error toast
Non-admin users do not see any of these actions (hidden, not disabled)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend Edit Pelada settings and Delete Pelada
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to edit pelada settings; as the creator, I want to delete the pelada.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Edit' button visible only to admins; opens a modal/form pre-filled with current values; all creation fields are editable; on save calls updatePelada() and refetches pelada detail; toast on success/error
Optional image re-upload in edit form; if a new image is selected, calls uploadPeladaImage() after updatePelada()

'Delete Pelada' option visible only to the creator (compare JWT userId with creator); shows a confirmation dialog with warning text; on confirm calls deletePelada() then navigates to /home; toast on success
Submit/Delete buttons show loading state during API calls
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Backend minimal Daily entity stub for auto-creation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a minimal Daily entity so the auto-create scheduler can persist new daily sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: Daily JPA entity with fields: id (Long, auto), pelada (ManyToOne to Pelada, required), dailyDate (LocalDate, required), dailyTime (String, required), status (String, default 'SCHEDULED'), createdAt
DailyRepository extends JpaRepository with method: existsByPeladaAndDailyDate(Pelada pelada, LocalDate date)
Hibernate DDL auto creates the dailies table
Note: this is a stub — the Daily PRD will extend this entity with more fields (teams, matches, etc.)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend auto-create Daily scheduled job
userStories.description: As the system, I should auto-create a Daily 24 hours before each scheduled session when autoCreateDailyEnabled is true.
acceptanceCriteria: Spring @Scheduled job runs hourly (cron: '0 0 * * * *') to reliably catch the 24h window
Job queries all Peladas where autoCreateDailyEnabled = true
For each such Pelada, calculates the next occurrence of its dayOfWeek from now
If the next occurrence is within 24 hours AND no Daily already exists for that Pelada + date (use existsByPeladaAndDailyDate), creates a new Daily with status SCHEDULED and the pelada's timeOfDay
@EnableScheduling added to the main application class or a config class
Typecheck passes
